When two types with the same name are imported (e.g., Aspose.Words.Range and System.Range), the compiler cannot decide which one to use.
Resolve the ambiguity by either fully qualifying the type (Aspose.Words.Range) or creating an alias with the using directive.
Prefer using an alias (e.g., using AsposeRange = Aspose.Words.Range;) for readability when the type is used multiple times.
When both System.Range (C# 8+) and Aspose.Words.Range are in scope, the name 'Range' becomes ambiguous.
Resolve the ambiguity by using the fully‑qualified type name (Aspose.Words.Range) or by creating an alias (e.g., using AsposeRange = Aspose.Words.Range).
Resolve ambiguous type names by using 'var' or a fully‑qualified type (e.g., Aspose.Words.Range) when System.Range is also in scope.
